validation_rules.name: Parent–child I/O balance
validation_rules.definition: A decomposed process must have the same set of input and output data flows as its child DFD boundary flows, ensuring that the parent and child diagrams are balanced.
validation_rules.detect_logic: For each process with a child DFD, compute the set of input and output flow names on the parent node and compare it with the set of flows that cross the child diagram boundary; report imbalance if the sets differ.
validation_rules.note: If imbalance is found, check whether some flows are missing on the child diagram, unnecessary on the parent, or should be split/merged using the data dictionary.

validation_rules.name: Data balance within a process
validation_rules.definition: A process that has outputs but no inputs, or inputs that never contribute to outputs, indicates missing or superfluous data flows.
validation_rules.detect_logic: For each process, ensure there is at least one input and one output flow, and that each output can be traced to some input or data store; flag processes with zero inputs or zero outputs or with obviously unused inputs.
validation_rules.note: Add missing flows or remove unnecessary ones so that each process has meaningful inputs and outputs.

validation_rules.name: Data store flow direction
validation_rules.definition: The direction of data flows between processes and data stores must reflect read, write or update semantics; misuse of arrow direction leads to ambiguous models.
validation_rules.detect_logic: Check flows incident on data stores: flows from a process into a store are writes or updates; flows from a store to a process are reads; disallow flows that contradict these patterns unless explicitly modeled as read/write.
validation_rules.note: Correct any arrows whose direction does not match the intended read or write operation.

validation_rules.name: Uniform decomposition check
validation_rules.definition: Within the same DFD, having some processes already at basic level while others still require many additional layers of decomposition reduces readability.
validation_rules.detect_logic: Measure the decomposition depth for each major branch; if some branches are several levels deeper than others on the same diagram, mark the diagram as non-uniform.
validation_rules.note: Consider repartitioning and redrawing the parent diagram following the steps similar to the guideline table for redrawing parent DFDs.

validation_rules.name: Process naming pattern
validation_rules.definition: Process names should follow 'Verb + Noun' in a single language (Chinese or English), 2–32 characters.
validation_rules.detect_logic: Match against CN/EN pattern hints and check presence of known verbs from the verbs-to-process mapping; flag names that are only nouns or ambiguous.
validation_rules.error_message: Process name should be a verb+noun phrase, e.g., '验证密码', 'Update Order'.

validation_rules.name: Data flow naming pattern
validation_rules.definition: Data flow names should be verb phrases or data-object phrases that describe the movement, avoid raw IDs or generic words.
validation_rules.detect_logic: Require at least one verb or request/response keyword; disallow names like '数据' or '信息' alone.

validation_rules.name: Data store naming pattern
validation_rules.definition: Data store names should be nouns, optionally suffixed with '库'/'存储'/'Database'.
validation_rules.detect_logic: Disallow obvious verbs in data store names; prefer noun-only names with optional suffixes.

validation_rules.name: External entity naming pattern
validation_rules.definition: External entity names should be nouns or role names (User, Admin, 银行、支付网关), not actions.
validation_rules.detect_logic: Disallow typical action verbs in external entity names; allow role/organization/service nouns.

validation_rules.name: Language consistency
validation_rules.definition: Within a single diagram, prefer consistent language (Chinese or English) for element names to avoid mixed-language confusion.
validation_rules.detect_logic: Compute the proportion of CN vs EN tokens across names; if both exceed a threshold (e.g., >30% each), warn for consistency.
